body:
Title: CRA Form BC479 2025 British Columbia credits.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5010-tc/5010-tc-25e.txt
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/5010-tc/5010-tc-25e.pdf

BC479 numeric source values for encoded amounts, rates, thresholds, caps, and limits:
Value: 15000
Value: 18000
Value: 75
Value: 0.02
Value: 10000
Value: 0.10
Value: 120000
Value: 300000
Value: 400
Value: 64764
Value: 84764

CRA Form BC479 2025 calculates British Columbia refundable credits. Lines 1 to 6 calculate adjusted family net income by adding UCCB and RDSP repayments to net income, subtracting UCCB and RDSP income, flooring negative amounts at zero, and adding taxpayer and spouse or common-law partner amounts. Line 7 uses $18,000 if the taxpayer had a spouse or common-law partner on December 31, 2025, and $15,000 otherwise. Lines 9 and 10 each claim $75 for the basic and spouse sales tax credit amounts, line 12 multiplies income for the sales tax credit by 2%, and line 13 floors the sales tax credit at zero.

Line 14 caps Schedule BC(S12) home renovation expenses at $10,000 and multiplies by 10%. Line 20 caps unused venture capital credit plus January 1 to March 3 certificate credit at $120,000. Line 25 caps total venture capital credit at $300,000. Line 30 adds clean buildings credit and partnership credit. Line 34 adds the three training tax credit lines. Line 35 adds lines 16, 25, 26, 30, and 34.

The renter's tax credit section says that if adjusted family net income is less than $64,764, a qualifying taxpayer enters $400. If adjusted family net income is between $64,764 and $84,764, lines 38 to 44 calculate the credit by subtracting 2% of income above $64,764 from $400 and flooring the result at zero. If adjusted family net income is more than $84,764, the taxpayer cannot claim the credit. Line 45 adds line 36 and line 44 and enters British Columbia credits on T1 line 47900.
